description: Backend CRUD endpoints for calendar events (create + delete) and frontend API helpers
steps: In backend/app/main.py, add a POST /api/calendar/events endpoint that accepts JSON body {summary, start_time, duration_minutes, auth_token}. It should verify the auth token, then call mcp_client.call_tool('google-calendar', 'create_event', {...}) to create the event and return the result.

In backend/app/main.py, add a DELETE /api/calendar/events/{event_id} endpoint that accepts query param auth_token. It should verify the auth token, then call mcp_client.call_tool('google-calendar', 'delete_event', {'auth_token': auth_token, 'event_id': event_id}) and return success.
In frontend/lib/api.ts, add a createCalendarEvent(authToken, data: {summary, start_time, duration_minutes}) function that POSTs to /api/calendar/events with the body including auth_token.
In frontend/lib/api.ts, add a deleteCalendarEvent(authToken, eventId) function that calls DELETE /api/calendar/events/{eventId}?auth_token=...
In frontend/lib/api.ts, add a checkTokenScopes(accessToken) function that calls GET https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/tokeninfo?access_token=... and returns the parsed scope string (for TASK 2).
acceptance_criteria: backend/app/main.py imports correctly: python -c 'import app.main' succeeds
frontend/lib/api.ts exports createCalendarEvent, deleteCalendarEvent, and checkTokenScopes functions
Frontend build passes: cd frontend && pnpm build
findings: Both endpoints follow the existing GET /api/calendar/events pattern. The POST endpoint uses a dict body param (no Pydantic model) to keep it simple and consistent with the existing patterns in main.py. Frontend helpers follow the same getApiBase() + fetch pattern as existing functions.
